{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "023eb097-ce85-4362-84df-9772c3c8d36d",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Influence Lines\n",
    "\n",
    "This notebook demonstrates the use of `PyCBA` in obtaining influence lines for arbitary beams."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "193b90bd-2e85-4866-bd6b-f68140c04a26",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "First, the usual imports"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 1,
   "id": "ce40a9d1-c6fe-476f-a2b3-7dce5e1bc22f",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import pycba as cba\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "from IPython import display"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "e6e646b7-4dd0-4c71-95fe-06089e00509b",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Example 1 - Basic Use\n",
    "\n",
    "Here we take a reasonably complex beam configuration and seek two influence lines for it:"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "e05d065f-c344-4f1d-9af8-ecad1919eb72",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "execute_result",
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": "<IPython.core.display.Image object>"
     },
     "metadata": {
      "image/png": {
       "width": 800
      }
     },
     "execution_count": 3
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "display.Image(\"./images/il_ex_1.png\",width=800)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "81da8f02-88db-4a0c-a68b-2275ca314635",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "L = [5, 5, 10]\n",
    "EI = 30 * 600e7 * np.ones(len(L)) * 1e-6\n",
    "eType = [2, 1, 1]\n",
    "R = [-1, -1, 0, 0, -1, 0, -1, 0]"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "447a7393-bd79-4f7d-8845-f65af4679d7b",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "Next, create the `InfluenceLines` object from `PyCBA` by passing in the beam configuration information. Note that no loading information is requried (of course). And then we create the influence lines, passing in the `step` parameter - the increment in which the unit load is traversed across the structure."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "66a281f5-bc06-457c-9979-de32e7bc4b40",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "ils = cba.InfluenceLines(L, EI, R, eType)\n",
    "ils.create_ils(step=0.5)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "c8a1bb84465f",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": "The beam configuration — the fixed left support and the internal hinge — can be drawn from the underlying analysis (`ils.ba.beam`). We pass `loads=[]` to show the bare structure, since the influence-line solver leaves a unit probe load on the beam:"
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "id": "b634d82681bb",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-22T08:06:05.105453Z",
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-22T08:06:05.105589Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-22T08:06:05.200052Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-22T08:06:05.200706Z"
    }
   },
   "source": [
    "ils.ba.plot_beam(loads=[]);"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "display_data",
     "data": {
      "text/plain": "<Figure size 1000x320 with 1 Axes>",
      "image/png": ""
     },
     "metadata": {}
    }
   ],
   "execution_count": 5
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "fa4384c1-ae50-4fda-8603-f670ddd61740",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "`PyCBA` \"brute force\" calculates the influence lines, and so all are now available with no further computation necessary. Here we just plot two examples on axes that we have pre-created:\n",
    "- the shear force (`\"V\"`) at $15$ m along the beam\n",
    "- the vertical reaction (`\"R\"`) at $0$ m along the beam"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 6,
   "id": "940572fb-1005-4dc6-8c6f-66726282af9c",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "display_data",
     "data": {
      "text/plain": "<Figure size 1000x600 with 2 Axes>",
      "image/png": ""
     },
     "metadata": {}
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "fig, axs = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(10, 6))\n",
    "ils.plot_il(15.0, \"V\", axs[0])\n",
    "ils.plot_il(0.0, \"R\", axs[1])"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "63fff591-9589-4940-a696-6255a091bc83",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "If the actual influence abcissa ($x$) and ordinates (denoted $\\eta$) are required, they can be got as follows:"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 7,
   "id": "0767d3b0-769a-49fc-803c-cdf7560def80",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "stream",
     "name": "stdout",
     "text": "[ 0.          0.0018125   0.007       0.0151875   0.026       0.0390625\n  0.054       0.0704375   0.088       0.1063125   0.125       0.1186875\n  0.112       0.1045625   0.096       0.0859375   0.074       0.0598125\n  0.043       0.0231875   0.         -0.02684375 -0.05725    -0.09103125\n -0.128      -0.16796875 -0.21075    -0.25615625 -0.304      -0.35409375\n  0.59375     0.53971875  0.484       0.42678125  0.36825     0.30859375\n  0.248       0.18665625  0.12475     0.06246875  0.        ]\n"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "(x, eta) = ils.get_il(15.0, \"V\")\n",
    "print(eta)"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "bccbfbc7-9ac3-4ce9-8ed0-398c0249050a",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Example 2 - Beam Strings\n",
    "\n",
    "The definition of beam configurations for influence lines can be made simpler because:\n",
    "\n",
    "1. no loading information is required\n",
    "2. deflection influence lines are rarely sought, and so the flexural rigidity can be assumed as a nominal value\n",
    "\n",
    "Thus, with the assumption that the beam is prismatic, beams can be more easily defined for influence line generation by a \"beam string\". Please refer to the documentation for the defintions of terms for the beam string (`pycba.utils.parse_beam_string`).\n",
    "\n",
    "Here we define a four-span beam (30 m equal spans) with fixed end supports and a hinge at the middle using a beam string, and parse it for the inputs to the `InfluenceLines` object, from which we proceed as usual:"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 8,
   "id": "2c1f1a84-d31e-4968-a044-041659e1a33e",
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "output_type": "display_data",
     "data": {
      "text/plain": "<Figure size 1000x350 with 1 Axes>",
      "image/png": ""
     },
     "metadata": {}
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "beam_string = \"E30R30H30R30E\"\n",
    "(L, EI, R, eType) = cba.parse_beam_string(beam_string)\n",
    "ils = cba.InfluenceLines(L, EI, R, eType)\n",
    "ils.create_ils(step=0.05)\n",
    "ils.plot_il(0.0, \"R\")"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "metadata": {
  "kernelspec": {
   "display_name": "pycba",
   "language": "python",
   "name": "pycba"
  },
